items.title: After watching how several teams used second-round picks tonight, I'm left wanting more from the Bulls.
items.author: Drew
items.body_excerpt:
After watching how several teams used second-round picks tonight, I'm left wanting more from the Bulls.

Coming away with Kam Jones, future swap rights and cash after entering the night with Nos. 38 and 56 feels underwhelming.

items.title: June 24, 2004: With the fifth pick in the NBA Draft, the Washington Wizards select Devin Harris from the University of Wisconsin.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 24, 2004: With the fifth pick in the NBA Draft, the Washington Wizards select Devin Harris from the University of Wisconsin.

Harris was then traded—along with Christian Laettner and Jerry Stackhouse—to the Dallas Mavericks for Antawn Jamison.

items.title: Two picks before the Raptors draft and I have no clue who they'll take but I can pretty much guarantee one thing: He was the all-conference defensive player of the year in his conference or at least made the all-defensive team.
items.author: Doug Smith
items.body_excerpt:
Two picks before the Raptors draft and I have no clue who they'll take but I can pretty much guarantee one thing: He was the all-conference defensive player of the year in his conference or at least made the all-defensive team.

Oh, and he'll be 6-9 or so
